metadata.description: Breed restriction database: banned, restricted, and controlled dog breeds per P1 country + airline brachycephalic policies. Compiled from countries_pet_regulations.json and airline_pet_policies.json.
metadata.methodology: Aggregated from Block 5/6 country regulations database and Block 3/7 airline policies. Data sourced from official government legislation, airline published policies, and published guidelines.
metadata.content_use: Feeds breed-specific route pages, breed guide pages, and warning callouts on route pages where breed restrictions apply.

banned_types.name: Pit Bull Terrier
banned_types.exemption: Certificate of Exemption possible for existing dogs in UK

banned_types.name: Japanese Tosa
banned_types.exemption: Certificate of Exemption possible for existing dogs in UK

banned_types.name: Dogo Argentino
banned_types.exemption: Certificate of Exemption possible for existing dogs in UK

banned_types.name: Fila Brasileiro
banned_types.exemption: Certificate of Exemption possible for existing dogs in UK

UK.assessment_method: Type-based: a dog that 'substantially conforms' to the physical characteristics of a banned type can be classified as that type regardless of pedigree or registration
UK.mixed_breeds: Crosses of banned types are also prohibited
UK.notes: XL Bully added to restricted list in 2024 (England and Wales). Owners must obtain Certificate of Exemption, dog must be neutered, muzzled and on lead in public.

state_local_examples.status: Banned (county-level despite state preemption in other FL counties)

USA.notes: BSL varies enormously. Some states have banned BSL at state level. Import has no federal breed restriction. Check destination city/county before travel.
USA.import_impact: No breed-based import restrictions at federal level. Federal entry requirements are the same regardless of breed.

restricted_breeds.name: Pit Bull Terrier (and related types)
restricted_breeds.import_allowed: Varies by emirate. Generally restricted or requires special permit.

restricted_breeds.name: Rottweiler
restricted_breeds.status: restricted but not outright banned in most emirates

UAE.emirate_variations: Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Sharjah may each have different lists. Check with specific municipality.
UAE.notes: List may not be exhaustive. Verify with relevant municipality import permit office.

Australia.legislation: No federal breed ban for imports. State-level legislation.
Australia.restriction_type: State-level restrictions on ownership (not import ban)

state_restricted_breeds.name: Pit Bull Terrier / American Pit Bull Terrier
state_restricted_breeds.jurisdictions: Most states restrict or require registration

Australia.notes: Import is not restricted by breed at the federal/DAFF level. However, state authorities may restrict ownership. Check destination state before import.

Canada.legislation: No federal breed-specific legislation. Provincial and municipal BSL.

breeds: Pit Bull (American Pit Bull Terrier, American Staffordshire Terrier, Staffordshire Bull Terrier, and similar)
notable_bsl.status: Banned. Cannot own, breed, or import Pit Bulls into Ontario.

breeds: Previously banned Pit Bulls, law revised multiple times

Canada.notes: Ontario Pit Bull ban is the most significant: cannot import a Pit Bull into Ontario even as a personal pet. This affects Toronto routes specifically. Other provinces generally less restrictive.
Canada.import_impact: No federal import breed restriction, but provincial laws apply immediately upon arrival. Ontario is the critical one for pet transport.

Germany.restriction_type: Federal import ban + Bundeslaender additional lists
Germany.crosses: Crosses of all four breeds also banned from import

bundesland_additional_lists.description: Individual German states add breeds to their own 'dangerous dog' lists beyond the federal four
bundesland_additional_lists.notes: Bundesland lists vary. Some categorise breeds into Category 1 (banned/dangerous) and Category 2 (requires temperament test). Category 2 dogs may be imported with proof of temperament test results.

Germany.exemption: Bundesland-specific. Some allow import with prior approval and proof of non-dangerousness (temperament test/Wesenstest).
Germany.notes: Germany is the second strictest P1 country for breed restrictions (after France). Four breeds banned at the FEDERAL level specifically for import and transit. This directly impacts content for any route with Germany as destination.

France.restriction_type: Two-category system: Category 1 (outright ban) and Category 2 (restricted with permit)

category_1_banned.description: Attack dogs. BANNED from import. Cannot be acquired, sold, given away, or imported into France.

breeds.name: Pit Bull type (not registered in studbook/LOF)
breeds.notes: Unregistered dogs resembling Pit Bull type

category_1_banned.additional_rules: Category 1 dogs must be sterilised, muzzled and leashed in public, cannot access public transport, cannot be in common areas of apartment buildings

category_2_restricted.description: Guard and defence dogs. Restricted but CAN be imported with conditions.

breeds.name: American Staffordshire Terrier (registered in studbook/LOF)
breeds.conditions: Permis de detention required, civil liability insurance, muzzle and leash in public

breeds.name: Rottweiler (registered or not)
breeds.conditions: Permis de detention required, civil liability insurance, sterilisation if not registered in studbook

breeds.name: Tosa (registered in studbook)
breeds.conditions: Permis de detention required, civil liability insurance

permit_requirements: Permis de detention (detention permit) from local mairie

France.notes: France has the strictest breed framework of all P1 countries. Category 1 is an absolute import ban. Category 2 requires significant paperwork and ongoing compliance. Route pages with France as destination MUST prominently flag breed restrictions.

Singapore.legislation: Animals and Birds (Dog Licensing and Control) Rules

prohibited_breeds.name: Pit Bull Terrier (including American Pit Bull Terrier, American Staffordshire Terrier, Staffordshire Bull Terrier)
Akita
prohibited_breeds.notes: Unusual restriction. Not commonly banned globally.

prohibited_breeds.name: Neapolitan Mastiff
prohibited_breeds.notes: Unusual restriction. Not commonly banned globally.

Singapore.crosses: Crosses of prohibited breeds are also prohibited

hdb_restrictions.description: HDB (Housing Development Board) public housing (approximately 80% of Singapore's population) has additional breed restrictions
hdb_restrictions.rules: Only approved small dog breeds allowed in HDB flats. Maximum 1 dog per HDB household.
hdb_restrictions.approved_breeds: HDB maintains a list of 62 approved breeds plus mixed breeds under 15 kg
hdb_restrictions.cats: Cats permitted in HDB flats since September 2024 (previously prohibited). Maximum 2 cats per household.

Singapore.notes: 8 breeds prohibited from import. Akita and Neapolitan Mastiff restrictions are unusual compared to other countries. HDB housing rules further constrain which dogs can practically be relocated to Singapore.

prohibited_fighting_dogs.name: Pit Bull Terrier (including American Pit Bull Terrier)

known_large_dogs.description: Dogs classified as 'known large dogs' (over 20 kg in adulthood) face additional requirements
requirements: Must be held on a leash not exceeding 2 metres in public
Must be under control of person over 16 years old
known_large_dogs.notes: Large dogs are not banned from import but face additional handling requirements

Hong_Kong.housing_restrictions: Many housing estates (public and private) have breed and size restrictions. Verify with estate management before relocating a pet.
Hong_Kong.notes: Four fighting dog types prohibited. Similar to UK list. Additional practical restrictions in housing estates.

municipal_examples.status: Some municipalities require special permits or have bylaws

South_Africa.notes: No national import breed restriction. South Africa is one of the least restrictive P1 countries for breed-based import. Municipal bylaws may apply at destination.

airline_brachycephalic_restrictions.description: Brachycephalic (snub-nosed) breed restrictions across airlines. Virtually all airlines restrict or ban these breeds from cargo holds due to respiratory distress risks at altitude and during temperature extremes.

complete_cargo_ban.description: Airlines that outright ban all brachycephalic breeds from cargo transport year-round
complete_cargo_ban.notes: Some may have seasonal exceptions or cabin alternatives

seasonal_cargo_ban.description: Airlines that ban brachycephalic breeds from cargo during hot months only
airlines: Japan Airlines (English Bulldog year-round, French Bulldog May-Oct)
seasonal_cargo_ban.hot_months: Typically May-October in northern hemisphere, November-March for southern hemisphere/Gulf routes

accepted_with_restrictions.description: Airlines that accept brachycephalic breeds in cargo with additional requirements
airlines: Delta (domestic only, with health cert within 10 days)
typical_requirements: Additional health certificate dated within 10 days

cabin_alternative.description: Some brachycephalic breeds can fly in cabin if small enough for carrier
cabin_alternative.notes: Cabin transport is the primary viable option for small brachycephalic breeds on long-haul international flights

content_opportunity.search_demand: High. 'Can French Bulldogs fly?', 'flying with a bulldog', 'brachycephalic breed airline rules' are high-volume queries.
page_types: Breed-specific guide pages (e.g. 'Flying with a French Bulldog: Airline Guide')
Route-specific brachycephalic callouts (embedded in route pages)
Comparison tables: which airlines accept which brachycephalic breeds
content_opportunity.notes: French Bulldog is the most searched brachycephalic breed for air travel. English Bulldog has the most restrictions. Pug and Boston Terrier are mid-tier in both search volume and restriction severity.

breed_restriction_severity_ranking.description: Overall ranking of P1 countries by breed import restriction severity

ranking.reason: Category 1 breeds outright banned from import. Category 2 requires permit, insurance, sterilisation, behavioural assessment, and training.
4 breeds banned from import at federal level. Bundeslaender add 5-10+ more breeds each.
8 breeds prohibited from import. Additional HDB housing restrictions limit most residents to small breeds only.
4 types banned. Type-based assessment (not registration). XL Bully added 2024.
4 fighting dog types prohibited. Housing estate restrictions add practical constraints.
8+ breeds restricted. Varies by emirate. Some breeds may get special permits.
No federal ban, but Ontario Pit Bull ban is significant (affects Toronto routes).
No federal import breed ban. State-level ownership restrictions exist.
No federal BSL. State/local BSL varies. No import breed restriction.
No national import breed restriction. Municipal bylaws may apply.

common_restricted_breed_matrix.description: Which commonly restricted breeds are banned/restricted in which P1 countries

restricted: France (Cat 2 if registered as Am Staff)

banned_from_import: France (Cat 1 if unregistered, Cat 2 if registered)

akita.notes: Singapore's Akita ban is unusual. Important for Japan-Singapore route content.

restricted: UK (if assessed as Pit Bull type)
